I notice myself referring to other people by their full name more often recently. I suspect that when I want to refer to a person as a subject itself which I have a relation with I will refer to them by their first name i.e. I spoke to John today. However when I refer to them as the kind of essence of their self and not particularly them as a subject I'll use their full name i.e. the object which is their essence purely stripped from my subjective relationship with them. And I also find that I have a lot of these Full Name relationships wherein I transfigure the essence into this twisted deified thing and the person becomes literally Jesus Christ and I can hardly say its a relationship at all. Astute readers will know who and what I'm talking about.
Also do not let the Psychotic Caprice symptomatic of late nights/drunkenness/yearning/sleep deprivation/boredom make decisions for you. It's really bad

you like it. Mayve its pretentious enough... Neologism for Postpostmodern (in reflection its kind of actually postpostpostmodern) Oliver and I coined recently - orthomodern. Let me know if  Orthomodern I think it does a good job of kind of capturing the whole techno-nihilist vibe. I want it to encapsulate the nihilistic rennaisance of post-ai post-shorts post-trump era. And the epistemic armageddon that comes with it. Like the whole thing. The ortho- part is supposed to be a clipping of orthogonal seperate from the typical meaning of ortho- (which I think in of itself is a meta-commentary) I think it kind of means like, orthogonal to human progress, to the linearity of human advancement and culture; if x axis is time and y axis is progess, orthomodern is the vertical asymptote. Also. Its kind of steeped in this second-order irony where its being sincere or ironic is in of itself ironic of sincere. Do you know what I mean? This is what the internet is now.

Worst and best things about myself (uncategorized):

- I am a rather unreliable person (I tend not to do things that I have an obligation to do because I don't want to)
- I do not leave my room often
- I'm very good at remembering experiences and thoughts
- I'm a bit fat
- I'm very good at disciplining myself to do things that I enjoy now
- I'm very smart and learn things very quickly
- I have a horrible addiction to consuming a lot of terrible things in short intervals
- I am bad at meeting new people
- I am determined and often succeed when I plan ahead
- I am a kind person and I care for others
- I am a slob and have poor organization sometimes
- I am well versed in strange trivia
- I have a variety of interesting hobbies and quirks
- I have poor management of the sensitivities and passions of my emotion
- I am creative and can solve difficult and nuanced problems

Extremely strange phenomena I’ve observed: When I want to make something really pretty and I care a lot about how it will look, I’ll give it a couple tries (e.g. I’ll write a word / take a picture / draw a doodle a couple of times and pick the best one) and very very often the first one is the best one. I think this is pretty absurd and interesting though I’d suspect it has more to do with cognitive biases than anything (devise an experiment!)

Unfortunately every single macro description of the world is wrong. And the only way to truly understand anything is to look Really. Really. Really. Closely. Newtonian mechanics are really just quantum mechanics at a scale without wavelength interference. Macroeconomics is the aggregate of individual choice etc. etc. but you can’t truly understand how a ball falls or why the market moves without first understanding what jimmyjohn from north dakota knows and thinks and desires or how a singular atom behaves. it’s not even about reconciling these two lenses but at least. Being able to make inferences with wcale. Thats what I hate the most about anything to do with humans especially. Like history politics sociology economics psychology religion philosophy whatever it is. Macro lenses blur the ultimate motivation for all human activity… desire for… whatever it may be. It’s frustrating to learn things at such a scale and neglect the jarring imperfections we are too embarassed to admit to. And I think this is the ultimate paradox of democracy, that individual choice MATTERS, but in that each desire is dangerously fallable and malleable. by understanding individuals. we can be turned into groups, and that is when we lose any semblance of autonomy

Really funny quotes from the wikipedia entry about the wittgenstein family (The one with Ludwig) Honestly they are so funny and amazing in a really sort of disturbing morbid way. I really must share them.

From Paul to a young Ludwig, while practicing the piano: “I cannot play when you are in the house, as I feel your skepticism seeping towards me from under the door!” 

“I ought to have ... become a star in the sky. Instead of which I have remained stuck on earth.”

Anyways three of five brothers committed suicide. The Wittgensteins were the second richest family in europe, only behind the Rothschilds, who were also Jewish. Not saying theres a pattern or anything.

Well at least for me I would imagine that the reason why liking someone particularly is so painful that I actively avoid liking anyone to any extent… would be probably something like an external manefestation of my personal inadequacy. Me not feeling deserving of liking people. But who fucking cares about me. Anyways from my notes app these are the people I liked in my life. A bit embarassing but when I was 13 years old or something in middle school I really liked this one girl I was very close with and then One random day I told her over discord and I was so embarassed I never felt the same again. Andthat was particularly terrible. The rest are a bit self explanatory. I would say I was only particularly infatuated with in my life was my middle school friend and since then everyone else that I’ve liked was in a sort of doting idolizing way that wasnt really … romantic and moreso an aesthetic fantasy. Keen leon zhou fans will notice the intentional omission of a certain person but. It’s also important to note that even throughout our relationship I would seriously consider categorizing her rather as an aesthetic fantasy than a helpless infatuation in that fairy tale sense.

Context is such an important part of art…I think that in every part of human communication there is some amount of context that is to be had (i.e. empiricism)… at the very least in language there is the understanding of words and in art which is kind of a language there is the deciphering of visual elements… art is the aesthetic capturing of meaning and so naturally without the context of the experience or the context of the viewer (which are more similar than you may think) art cannot have meaning… and so it upsets me that some things are denied meaning because its context is stripped from it. Even for example if I’m me and I make a joke it would be more funny than if a comedian did because I’m such a dry boring guy that the context of me would make it funnier than if a funny guy you expect to be funny said it. Or if Hitler drew something it would be a more meaningful piece of art than if some random guy did because its something beautiful an ugly guy made. or if the Sistine Chapel ceiling was just in some dude’s bedroom… Yeah… Lmk if u agree
